name: Penyal d'Honor — circular from Palma de Mallorca via Bunyola — Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana
name_original: Penyal d'Honor — circular desde Palma de Mallorca por Bunyola — Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana
region_intro: Mountain biking around Palma offers diverse terrain, from the challenging mountainous landscapes of the nearby Serra de Tramuntana to more accessible coastal paths and urban forests. The region features rocky and sometimes sandy trails, providing a technical feel for experienced riders, alongside gentler routes through Mediterranean Holm Oak and Pine forests. Natural features like the Na Burguesa hills and Bellver Forest contribute to a varied riding experience. This blend of terrain caters to a wide range of skill levels, making Palma a versatile destination for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Palma The most popular mountain bike trail is Castillo de Bellver — circular from Palma de Mallorca via the collado de sa Creu, a 13.7 miles (22.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around Bellver Castle and the Collado de sa Creu. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Penyal d'Honor — circular from Palma de Mallorca via Bunyola — Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana, a difficult 32.2 miles (51.8 km) path. This challenging route leads through the mountainous terrain of the Serra de Tramuntana. Local mountain bikers also love the Portixol Marina – Can Pastilla Beach loop from Bellver Castle, a 24.9 miles (40.2 km) trail leading through coastal areas and urban forests, often completed in about 2 hours 23 minutes. Mountain biking around Palma is defined by coastal paths, forested hills, and the rugged terrain of the Serra de Tramuntana. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to demanding mountain ascents. The routes in Palma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 480 reviews. More than 5000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Palma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Palma?
A: Palma offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 240 routes available. These range from easy coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Palma?
A: The terrain around Palma is quite diverse. You'll encounter rocky and sometimes sandy trails, especially in the more technical areas. Routes also traverse Mediterranean Holm Oak and Pine forests, and some coastal paths are smoother. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009859627/penyal-d-honor-circular-from-palma-de-mallorca-via-bunyola-paraje-natural-de-la-sierra-de-tramuntana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Tramuntana&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers significant climbs and descents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Palma?
A: Yes, Palma has options for beginners. While many trails offer a technical feel, there are gentler routes, particularly through urban forests like Bellver Forest and forestry roads in Na Burguesa. Out of the over 240 routes, 38 are rated as easy.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009859627/penyal-d-honor-circular-from-palma-de-mallorca-via-bunyola-paraje-natural-de-la-sierra-de-tramuntana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyal d'Honor — circular from Palma de Mallorca via Bunyola — Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 32.2-mile (51.8 km) path with significant elevation gains through the mountainous terrain of the Serra de Tramuntana. There are 71 routes rated as difficult in the region.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Palma?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Palma are circular. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010398651/castillo-de-bellver-circular-from-palma-de-mallorca-via-the-collado-de-sa-creu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Bellver — circular from Palma de Mallorca via the collado de sa Creu&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the area around Bellver Castle. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38262184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portixol Marina – Can Pastilla Beach loop from Bellver Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers coastal and urban forest riding.

Q: What are the best times of year for mountain biking in Palma?
A: Palma's mild climate makes it an attractive destination for mountain biking throughout much of the year, especially for those looking to escape harsher winters. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Palma?
A: While mountain biking near Palma, you can encounter diverse natural features and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/687473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Tramuntana&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning mountain views. You might also pass by historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/79390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Cura, Puig de Randa&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy scenic trails through areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/357199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrent de Valldemossa&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer viewpoints overlooking the coast or the city.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, many areas popular for mountain biking, such as those near Bellver Forest or the starting points for routes into Na Burguesa, typically have parking options. For routes starting directly in Palma, public parking facilities are available.

Q: How do other mountain bikers rate the trails around Palma?
A: The mountain bike trails around Palma are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 480 reviews. Riders often praise the variety of terrain, from challenging mountain paths to scenic coastal routes, and the beautiful natural surroundings.

Q: Are there mountain biking routes accessible by public transport from Palma?
A: Palma serves as a central hub, and many routes start directly within or very close to the city, making them accessible by public transport or a short ride from urban areas. For routes further afield in the Serra de Tramuntana, local bus services can often get you closer to starting points, though checking specific routes and bike transport policies is advisable.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in Palma?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on established trails around Palma. However, always respect local regulations, especially when riding through natural parks or protected areas, to ensure you are on designated paths.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, especially on routes closer to Palma or passing through towns and villages, you can often find amenities like cafes, restaurants, and shops. Coastal routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33823451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Pastilla Beach – View of Playa de Palma loop from Son Fuster&lt;/a&gt;, are particularly well-served with options for refreshments.
tips: The views of the valley and the western coast of the Mediterranean Sea from the path of sa Comuna.
The view is simply unbeatable. You can see as far as Bunyola.

waypoints.name: View of the Bunyola Valley and the Western Mediterranean coast
